plain_definition: The Payment Services Act 2019's defined term for what is commonly called a cryptocurrency -- a digital unit of value not denominated in or pegged to any currency, usable as a medium of exchange and transferable, storable or tradeable electronically. It is the PS Act's licensing-scope trigger for 'digital payment token service' (see the dpt-service entry), and is distinct from an 'e-money' instrument (denominated in and pegged to a currency) and from a capital-markets-product token, which instead falls under the Securities and Futures Act 2001.
formal_definition: This entry could not directly fetch the Payment Services Act 2019's own section 2(1)/First Schedule 'digital payment token' definition -- Singapore Statutes Online (sso.agc.gov.sg) returned a live-verified HTTP 403 CloudFront block on every path tried on the date of drafting (2026-07-13) -- so the general shape of the PS Act definition given in plain_definition rests on wide, consistent secondary/law-firm corroboration (multiple independent Singapore law-firm summaries and a U.S. Library of Congress Global Legal Monitor note), not on independently fetched statute text. A related but textually distinct 'digital payment token' definition, confirmed by direct fetch of IRAS's e-Tax Guide 'GST: Digital Payment Tokens' (Third Edition, 30 January 2026, iras.gov.sg), applies for Goods and Services Tax exemption purposes and states a DPT 'is not pegged by its issuer to any currency' (Glossary, s.3.2). This project has not confirmed that the PS Act's own licensing-scope DPT definition contains an identical exclusion, so the GST Act definition quoted here should not be assumed word-for-word identical to the PS Act's own definition.
